name: Write-what-where Condition
description: An attacker gains the ability to write an arbitrary value to an arbitrary memory location, typically as a result of a buffer overflow corrupting heap metadata or function pointers, enabling complete control over program execution.
detection_pattern: A TRANSFORM[buffer_overflow] or TRANSFORM[heap_corruption] causes a STORAGE[memory_write] operation where both the destination address and the written value are attacker-influenced, without a CONTROL[write_protection] such as ASLR, stack canaries, or safe unlinking that prevents arbitrary writes
